attachment.command: sh -c 'if ! command -v entire >/dev/null 2>&1; then printf "%s\n" "{\"systemMessage\":\"\\n\\nEntire CLI is enabled but not installed or not on PATH.\\nInstallation guide: https://docs.entire.io/cli/installation#installation-methods\"}"; exit 0; fi; exec entire hooks claude-code session-start'

attachment.content: Entire CLI will link this conversation to your next commit.

- verify-all: Run the full check + clippy sweep across the workspace AND the WASM plugin (panopt-zellij). Use after non-trivial edits, before declaring work done, or when asked to verify the repo builds cleanly. Captures the dual-target dance (workspace builds for host, panopt-zellij builds only for wasm32-wasip1) so you don't have to remember the right manifest-path/target each time.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: Now let me explore the codebase to understand the cockpit layout and pane handling.

input.prompt:
I'm planning work on a panopt todo:

> Title: "prevent closing last right-side pane"
> Body: "if it's closed, there's no way to make a new one!!"

panopt is a Rust workspace at /home/gregz/cp/panopt. It has a Zellij sidebar plugin (`crates/panopt-zellij`, a WASM plugin) and a `panopt` CLI launcher that drives Zellij. The cockpit layout has a left sidebar (the plugin) and panes on the right side where agents/Claude Code sessions run.

I need to understand:

1. How is the cockpit's Zellij layout defined — what is the "right side" structurally? Is it a tab, a pane group, a stack? Look for layout files (KDL) and any Rust code that creates/spawns panes on the right.

2. How are right-side panes created and closed? Find:
   - Code that spawns new agent panes (likely uses Zellij's `open_command_pane` or similar Zellij plugin API).
   - Any existing "intercept close" / "suppress" logic. CLAUDE.md notes: "The plugin never closes panes, only suppresses them. Swap-in-place is the model: a suppressed pane keeps running hidden, the user owns its lifecycle." — find where this suppression happens.
   - Any Zellij event handlers (`PaneClosed`, `PaneAdded`, tab/pane updates) in the plugin.

3. What Zellij plugin API surface is available for: detecting when a pane is about to close, counting panes in a region/tab, focusing a pane, or preventing a close. Look at the plugin's Cargo.toml for the zellij-tile version and how the plugin uses it. Look in the plugin source for `update()` event handling.

4. Where would the fix live — the WASM plugin reacting to events, or the launcher pre-arranging layout, or both? Identify the specific files and the key functions involved.

Please report back with:
- The structure of the cockpit layout (with file paths).
- How panes are spawned and how/where existing suppression works (with file paths + relevant function names + brief snippets if useful).
- The Zellij event/API surface the plugin already uses for pane lifecycle.
- Your best guess at where the fix should go and why.

Keep it focused — I need enough to write a plan, not a full audit. Report in under 600 words.

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have the full picture. Let me write up my findings:

## Summary

I've analyzed the panopt codebase to understand the cockpit layout, pane lifecycle, and current suppression/protection mechanisms. Here are the key findings:

### 1. **Cockpit Layout Structure**
- **File**: `/home/gregz/cp/panopt/panopt.kdl` (static reference) and `/home/gregz/cp/panopt/crates/panopt/src/up.rs` lines 28–94 (generated template)
- **Structure**: 
  - Left sidebar: 5 fixed plugin panes (25% column) stacked vertically in a horizontal split—todos, agents, terminals, commands, scratchpads. Each is pinned to a fixed proportion.
  - Right side: Single `children` slot (a pane placeholder) that initially holds one `panopt _viewer` pane spawned at boot.
  - The right side is NOT a tab group, pane stack, or protected container—it's a named pane slot that can be freely split/closed by the user.

### 2. **Pane Spawning & Suppression**
- **Spawning**: `/home/gregz/cp/panopt/crates/panopt-zellij/src/main.rs` uses `open_command_pane()` and `open_command_pane_floating()` (zellij-tile 0.44 API) to spawn panes.
- **Suppression mechanism** (lines 373–514):
  - Each `PaneInfo` struct carries a `suppressed: bool` flag ingested from Zellij's `PaneManifest` via `PaneUpdate` event (line 623).
  - `pane_is_visible()` (line 1175) checks `!p.suppressed`; a suppressed pane still runs but is hidden.
  - `first_suppressed_viewer()` (line 1186) finds hidden viewers to reuse them on a swap.
  - On selection, the plugin calls `open_command_pane_in_place_of_pane_id(slot, ...)` (line 1928) to swap-in-place, suppressing whatever was there.

### 3. **Zellij Event & API Surface**
- **Version**: zellij-tile 0.44
- **Events handled**: `Event::PaneUpdate(manifest)` (line 623) is the primary lifecycle hook—it carries a flattened manifest of all panes, their IDs, roles, suppression state, and tab/title info.
- **No direct `PaneClosed` handler**: The plugin relies on `PaneUpdate` diffs to detect when a pane vanishes from the manifest (lines 1155–1163 clean up stale slot/search pane references).
- **Active pane detection** (lines 2193–2243): `pane_active()` filters out exited/viewer panes and identifies agents, commands, or terminals still running.
- **Pane counting**: `active_in_tab()` and `active_anywhere()` count active (non-idle) panes by tab or globally.

### 4. **Close-Gate Logic (Current Protection)**
- **Files**: Lines 2065–2110 and 1740–1755 in `/home/gregz/cp/panopt/crates/panopt-zellij/src/main.rs`
- **How it works**:
  - `gate_close_focus()` (line 2065): Intercepts close-focus-pane requests (from keybinds in `up.rs`). Refuses if a sidebar plugin pane is focused (absolute refusal). If a right-side pane is focused and it's active (running), spawns a floating dialog offering "close anyway" override.
  - `gate_close_tab()` (line 2085): Intercepts close-tab requests. Refuses if any active process/agent/terminal is in the tab.
  - The gate shows a `panopt _close-gate` dialog (line 2137) only when there's active work to lose.
- **Limitation**: The gate has no logic to prevent the *last* visible right-side pane from closing; it only guards against losing *active* work.

### 5. **Where the Fix Should Live**
The fix should go in **two places**:

1. **Plugin detection** (`/home/gregz/cp/panopt/crates/panopt-zellij/src/main.rs`, `gate_close_focus()` around line 2065):
   - Count visible (non-suppressed) right-side panes by filtering `self.panes` for `!p.suppressed` and excluding the five left sidebar plugin panes.
   - If closing would leave zero visible right-side panes, refuse with reason like `"cannot close last content pane"` (use `refuse_gate()` line 2260).
   - This catch is lightweight and runs before the gate dialog spawns.

2. **Launcher pre-arrangement** (optional, defensive): Ensure `panopt up` (in `up.rs`) always spawns at least one initial viewer pane so a new cockpit never starts with zero right-side panes.

**Why split**: The plugin gate prevents accidental deletion at runtime; the launcher ensures a fresh cockpit never reaches a broken state.

content.content:
370	    /// exists; until then the sidebar shows only live instances, same as the
371	    /// pre-V6 roster view.
372	    processes: Vec<ProcessRow>,
373	    /// Live (and suppressed) content panes flattened from Zellij's manifest.
374	    panes: Vec<PaneRow>,
375	
376	    /// Items currently shown by this mode, rebuilt on every change. Always a
377	    /// single flat list; no sections.
378	    items: Vec<Item>,
379	    /// Status filter the Todos pane applies when building [`Self::items`].
380	    /// Cycled with `f` / `F`; held across rebuilds so the user keeps their
381	    /// view as the projection changes underneath. Ignored by every other
382	    /// mode.
383	    todo_filter: TodoFilter,
384	    /// Primary sort axis for the Todos pane. Cycled with `1` / `!`; default
385	    /// [`TodoSort::PriorityDesc`]. Held in-memory only - reset to the
386	    /// default on every Zellij restart, same as [`Self::todo_filter`].
387	    todo_sort_1: TodoSort,
388	    /// Secondary sort axis, applied as a stable tiebreaker. Cycled with
389	    /// `2` / `@`. Defaults to [`TodoSort::CreatedAsc`] (oldest first) which
390	    /// together with the priority-desc level 1 surfaces the highest-priority
391	    /// oldest-open todo at the top.
392	    todo_sort_2: TodoSort,
393	
394	    /// Index of the keyboard-selected item. Stays in `0..items.len()`.
395	    cursor: usize,
396	    /// Index of the topmost item rendered in the visible window. The window
397	    /// is `last_rows - 1` items (one row reserved for the title).
398	    scroll: usize,
399	    /// Last `rows` value passed to [`PanoptPane::render`]. Cached so the key
400	    /// and mouse handlers can clamp scroll using a single source of truth.
401	    last_rows: usize,
402	    /// Last `cols` value passed to [`PanoptPane::render`]. Cached so
403	    /// [`PanoptPane::frame_title`] can fit the title to the pane's width
404	    /// (dropping the counts segment when the pane is too narrow to also
405	    /// show the filter/sort label, rather than letting Zellij truncate the
406	    /// middle of the label - the part the user most wants to read).
407	    last_cols: usize,
408	
409	    /// This plugin's own pane id, learned at load - used to return focus to
410	    /// the plugin pane after a swap.
411	    plugin_pane: Option<PaneId>,
412	    /// The pane occupying the designated content slot: the pane a selection
413	    /// swaps against. It is the last non-plugin pane focused before any
414	    /// plugin pane took focus, updated in place whenever the plugin swaps
415	    /// the slot itself.
416	    slot_pane: Option<PaneId>,
417	    /// The floating `panopt search` popup pane when one is open, so the
418	    /// plugin can close it from its own dispatch (avoiding a race with the
419	    /// search CLI's own `zellij action close-pane` against the focus-shift
420	    /// that lands on the viewer when a selection swaps). Cleared on close.
421	    search_pane: Option<PaneId>,
422	    /// How many ad-hoc agents this instance has numbered. Only meaningful in
423	    /// the Todos (gatekeeper) pane, which is the only pane that handles the
424	    /// `panopt:spawn-agent` pipe; other panes pick up labels from the
425	    /// projection file at [`AGENT_LABELS_PATH`].
426	    next_agent: u32,
427	    /// Counter for allocating unique routing slot names for viewer panes the
428	    /// plugin spawns. Combined with [`Mode::letter`] so two plugin instances
429	    /// cannot allocate the same name. The boot viewer keeps its `--slot main`
430	    /// from the layout.
431	    next_viewer_slot: u32,
432	    /// Sidebar label for each agent pane, keyed by terminal pane id. The
433	    /// gatekeeper writes user-supplied labels here and projects the map to
434	    /// [`AGENT_LABELS_PATH`]; every other pane reads from that file. Never
435	    /// participates in ordering - agent rows order by pane id (creation
436	    /// order), so a label change cannot reshuffle the list.
437	    agent_labels: BTreeMap<u32, String>,
438	
439	    /// Stable PANopt agent id for each cockpit-spawned agent pane, keyed by
440	    /// terminal pane id. Populated when [`Self::spawn_agent_pane`] launches
441	    /// `_agent --id <id>`; used by [`Self::sync_agent_labels`] to call
442	    /// `_agent-leave` on the daemon as that id the moment its pane closes,
443	    /// so the registry entry and any advisory locks clear without waiting
444	    /// on the idle sweep (todo #83). Independent of `agent_labels`: the
445	    /// label is what the human sees, this is what the daemon knows.
446	    agent_pane_ids: BTreeMap<u32, String>,
447	
448	    /// Whether any plugin pane is currently the focused pane in its tab.
449	    /// Updated by [`PanoptPane::ingest_panes`] but only from a non-transient
450	    /// manifest: a transient `zellij action pipe` pane briefly steals focus
451	    /// while the close-gate pipes fly, and we must not let that flicker make
452	    /// the gate think the user has moved off the cockpit panes.
453	    /// Used by [`PanoptPane::gate_close_focus`] to refuse closing any plugin
454	    /// pane absolutely.
455	    sidebar_focused: bool,
456	    /// The tab position with a focused pane, derived from the same manifest
457	    /// snapshot that drives `sidebar_focused`. Scopes the CloseTab gate.
458	    focused_tab: Option<usize>,
459	    /// The last gate refusal: what was refused (a label), set when the gate
460	    /// blocks an action because active items would be lost. Surfaced through
461	    /// the pane's frame title so the user knows their keypress was
462	    /// intercepted. Cleared on the next successful navigation. Only the Todos
463	    /// pane runs the gate, so only the Todos pane ever sets this.
464	    last_gate_refusal: Option<String>,
465	    /// The frame title most recently pushed to Zellij via
466	    /// [`rename_plugin_pane`]. Kept so we only re-issue the host call when
467	    /// the title actually changes (gate refusal appears/clears, scroll
468	    /// position shifts, ...) rather than on every render tick.
469	    last_frame_title: String,
470	    /// Last terminal-pane title we pushed via [`rename_terminal_pane`],
471	    /// keyed by Zellij terminal pane id. Lets the Todos pane (the only one
472	    /// that titles right-pane terminals) re-issue the host call only when
473	    /// the title actually changes, rather than on every manifest tick.
474	    last_pane_titles: BTreeMap<u32, String>,
475	    /// Whether the initial preview has been shown on startup. Only the Todos
476	    /// pane drives the initial preview; the others remain idle until the
477	    /// user navigates them.
478	    initial_preview_done: bool,
479	    /// Counter for delaying the initial preview until the UI is ready.
480	    initial_preview_delay: u32,
481	    /// When `true`, the pane body shows the per-mode key cheat-sheet instead
482	    /// of the item list. Toggled by `?`; any other keypress dismisses it.
483	    /// Holds the same set of keys across modes so the UI never carries
484	    /// per-mode hint clutter - the help is the only place keys are listed.
485	    show_help: bool,
486	    /// The most recent `switch_to_mode` decision this plugin emitted:
487	    /// `Some(true)` after locking on content focus, `Some(false)` after
488	    /// restoring Normal on sidebar focus, `None` before any decision. Lets
489	    /// [`PanoptPane::ingest_panes`] fire `switch_to_mode` only on transitions
490	    /// and not on every manifest tick. Only the Todos pane writes this -
491	    /// see the gate in `ingest_panes`.
492	    last_emitted_locked: Option<bool>,
493	}
494	
495	/// A parsed `.panopt/processes.md` line. The line format is preserved from
496	/// the pre-V6 `roster.md` so the existing `[kind] #id label` parser still
497	/// works; any trailing `(from #N)` is dropped from `label`.
498	struct ProcessRow {
499	    kind: String,
500	    id: u64,
501	    label: String,
502	}
503	
504	/// A content pane flattened from Zellij's manifest.
505	struct PaneRow {
506	    id: PaneId,
507	    title: String,
508	    focused: bool,
509	    /// A suppressed pane is hidden but still running - swapped out of the
510	    /// slot by an earlier selection. Used by
511	    /// [`PanoptPane::route_pane_to_slot`] and
512	    /// [`PanoptPane::ensure_viewer_in_slot`] to tell whether a target pane
513	    /// is already on screen.
514	    suppressed: bool,
515	    exited: bool,
516	    role: PaneRole,
517	    /// For [`PaneRole::Viewer`] panes only: the `--slot X` token from the
518	    /// launch command, used as the routing file name
519	    /// `.panopt/.cockpit/viewer-<slot>.json`. `None` on any other role.
520	    viewer_slot: Option<String>,
521	    /// Tab position from the `PaneManifest`. Used by the CloseTab gate to
522	    /// scope active-item aggregation to a single tab.
523	    tab: usize,
524	}
525	
526	/// What a content pane is, derived from the command it was launched with.
527	#[derive(Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Debug)]
528	enum PaneRole {
529	    /// The shared `panopt _viewer` document pane.

content.text: I have enough to write the plan. Let me confirm one quick assumption about the launcher's keybinding setup before drafting.

input.content:
# Plan: todo #96 — prevent closing last right-side pane

## Context

Todo #96 (high priority, open):

> **prevent closing last right-side pane** — if it's closed, there's no way to make a new one!!

The cockpit layout has five plugin panes on the left (sidebar) and a single right-side slot for content (initially a `panopt _viewer`). Selections in the sidebar swap content in-place via `open_command_pane_in_place_of_pane_id`, which suppresses the prior occupant rather than closing it.

Today the close-focus gate (`gate_close_focus`) refuses an absolute close only when the **sidebar** is focused. When the right-side pane is focused it either closes immediately (if idle) or spawns the "active work would be lost" confirmation dialog (if running). Neither path checks whether this is the **last visible right-side pane** — so the user can land in a state with no content pane on the right and no obvious way to bring one back. The selection model assumes the slot exists, so once it's gone the sidebar can't easily swap a new viewer in.

The fix: in the same gate that already refuses sidebar closes, also refuse closing the right-side pane when doing so would leave zero visible (non-suppressed) terminal panes in the focused tab. Absolute refusal, no override dialog — consistent with the existing sidebar refusal and with the plugin's stated "never closes panes, only suppresses them" invariant.

## Change

Single-file change in the Zellij plugin.

### File: `crates/panopt-zellij/src/main.rs`

Add a helper and an early refusal branch in `gate_close_focus` (currently at lines 2065–2083).

1. **Helper** (place near `pane_active` / `active_in_tab`, ~line 2245):

   ```rust
   /// Count visible (non-suppressed, non-exited) terminal panes in the focused
   /// tab. The five plugin panes are PaneId::Plugin and excluded by construction
   /// so this returns the right-side pane count.
   fn visible_content_panes_in_focused_tab(&self) -> usize {
       let Some(tab) = self.focused_tab else { return 0 };
       self.panes
           .iter()
           .filter(|p| p.tab == tab)
           .filter(|p| matches!(p.id, PaneId::Terminal(_)))
           .filter(|p| !p.suppressed && !p.exited)
           .count()
   }
   ```

2. **Gate** in `gate_close_focus` (after the sidebar check, before the active-work dialog):

   ```rust
   if self.visible_content_panes_in_focused_tab() <= 1 {
       self.refuse_gate("cannot close the last content pane");
       return;
   }
   ```

   Place this between the `slot_pane` resolution and the `pane_active` dialog branch so the refusal short-circuits the dialog path. This means even if the pane holds active work, the user gets the "last pane" refusal — not the "close anyway" dialog — which is what we want (the dialog's confirm path calls `close_pane_with_id` directly and would otherwise bypass the check).

That's the entire fix.

## What we deliberately do NOT change

- **`gate_close_tab` / `gate_quit`**: closing a tab or quitting Zellij is a different intent. The todo is specifically about leaving the right side empty while staying in the cockpit. Not in scope.
- **Launcher (`up.rs`) layout / re-spawn**: the layout already spawns a boot viewer, and with the gate above a user can never reach the empty-right-side state. No defensive re-spawn needed.
- **Close-gate confirm dialog**: no change. The new refusal sits in front of the dialog spawn, so the dialog never opens when this is the last pane. `handle_gate_decision` (which executes `close_pane_with_id` on confirm) doesn't need its own check, because a dialog for this scenario will never be spawned.

## Reused existing surface

- `refuse_gate(&str)` — `main.rs:2260`, sets the gate-refusal message that appears in the plugin frame title.
- `self.focused_tab: Option<usize>` — `main.rs:458`, the tab the close request applies to (same scope `gate_close_tab` uses).
- `self.panes: Vec<PaneRow>` with `suppressed`/`exited`/`tab` fields — `main.rs:374,505–524`, the flattened pane manifest the plugin already maintains.
- `PaneId::Terminal(_)` vs `PaneId::Plugin(_)` — the natural sidebar-vs-content split, matching how `agent_pane_ids` is keyed (`main.rs:1222`).

## Verification

1. **Build**: `just check` (workspace + plugin) and `just clippy` per CLAUDE.md.
2. **Hot reload**: `just refresh` to rebuild and reload plugin + daemon + Zellij session per the user's [[feedback-rebuild-runtime-artifacts]] memory.
3. **Manual end-to-end**:
   - Fresh `panopt up` → one viewer on the right.
   - Focus the right-side viewer → press `Ctrl-W` / `x` (configured close-focus bind from `up.rs:145`).
   - **Expect**: pane stays open; plugin frame title reads `cannot close the last content pane`. No floating dialog.
   - Now Alt-n to split a second pane on the right → focus the second pane → close it.
   - **Expect**: closes normally (count was 2, now 1).
   - Focus the remaining pane → try to close again.
   - **Expect**: refusal again.
   - Sanity: with two right-side panes and a running command in one, close that running pane.
   - **Expect**: normal "close anyway?" confirm dialog (refusal only kicks in at count ≤ 1).
4. **Sidebar still refused absolutely**: focus any of the five plugin panes → press close → existing "cannot close the sidebar" refusal still works (regression check).

# Plan: todo #96 — prevent closing last right-side pane

## Context

Todo #96 (high priority, open):

> **prevent closing last right-side pane** — if it's closed, there's no way to make a new one!!

The cockpit layout has five plugin panes on the left (sidebar) and a single right-side slot for content (initially a `panopt _viewer`). Selections in the sidebar swap content in-place via `open_command_pane_in_place_of_pane_id`, which suppresses the prior occupant rather than closing it.

Today the close-focus gate (`gate_close_focus`) refuses an absolute close only when the **sidebar** is focused. When the right-side pane is focused it either closes immediately (if idle) or spawns the "active work would be lost" confirmation dialog (if running). Neither path checks whether this is the **last visible right-side pane** — so the user can land in a state with no content pane on the right and no obvious way to bring one back. The selection model assumes the slot exists, so once it's gone the sidebar can't easily swap a new viewer in.

The fix: in the same gate that already refuses sidebar closes, also refuse closing the right-side pane when doing so would leave zero visible (non-suppressed) terminal panes in the focused tab. Absolute refusal, no override dialog — consistent with the existing sidebar refusal and with the plugin's stated "never closes panes, only suppresses them" invariant.

## Change

Single-file change in the Zellij plugin.

### File: `crates/panopt-zellij/src/main.rs`

Add a helper and an early refusal branch in `gate_close_focus` (currently at lines 2065–2083).

1. **Helper** (place near `pane_active` / `active_in_tab`, ~line 2245):

   ```rust
   /// Count visible (non-suppressed, non-exited) terminal panes in the focused
   /// tab. The five plugin panes are PaneId::Plugin and excluded by construction
   /// so this returns the right-side pane count.
   fn visible_content_panes_in_focused_tab(&self) -> usize {
       let Some(tab) = self.focused_tab else { return 0 };
       self.panes
           .iter()
           .filter(|p| p.tab == tab)
           .filter(|p| matches!(p.id, PaneId::Terminal(_)))
           .filter(|p| !p.suppressed && !p.exited)
           .count()
   }
   ```

2. **Gate** in `gate_close_focus` (after the sidebar check, before the active-work dialog):

   ```rust
   if self.visible_content_panes_in_focused_tab() <= 1 {
       self.refuse_gate("cannot close the last content pane");
       return;
   }
   ```

   Place this between the `slot_pane` resolution and the `pane_active` dialog branch so the refusal short-circuits the dialog path. This means even if the pane holds active work, the user gets the "last pane" refusal — not the "close anyway" dialog — which is what we want (the dialog's confirm path calls `close_pane_with_id` directly and would otherwise bypass the check).

That's the entire fix.

## What we deliberately do NOT change

- **`gate_close_tab` / `gate_quit`**: closing a tab or quitting Zellij is a different intent. The todo is specifically about leaving the right side empty while staying in the cockpit. Not in scope.
- **Launcher (`up.rs`) layout / re-spawn**: the layout already spawns a boot viewer, and with the gate above a user can never reach the empty-right-side state. No defensive re-spawn needed.
- **Close-gate confirm dialog**: no change. The new refusal sits in front of the dialog spawn, so the dialog never opens when this is the last pane. `handle_gate_decision` (which executes `close_pane_with_id` on confirm) doesn't need its own check, because a dialog for this scenario will never be spawned.

## Reused existing surface

- `refuse_gate(&str)` — `main.rs:2260`, sets the gate-refusal message that appears in the plugin frame title.
- `self.focused_tab: Option<usize>` — `main.rs:458`, the tab the close request applies to (same scope `gate_close_tab` uses).
- `self.panes: Vec<PaneRow>` with `suppressed`/`exited`/`tab` fields — `main.rs:374,505–524`, the flattened pane manifest the plugin already maintains.
- `PaneId::Terminal(_)` vs `PaneId::Plugin(_)` — the natural sidebar-vs-content split, matching how `agent_pane_ids` is keyed (`main.rs:1222`).

## Verification

1. **Build**: `just check` (workspace + plugin) and `just clippy` per CLAUDE.md.
2. **Hot reload**: `just refresh` to rebuild and reload plugin + daemon + Zellij session per the user's [[feedback-rebuild-runtime-artifacts]] memory.
3. **Manual end-to-end**:
   - Fresh `panopt up` → one viewer on the right.
   - Focus the right-side viewer → press `Ctrl-W` / `x` (configured close-focus bind from `up.rs:145`).
   - **Expect**: pane stays open; plugin frame title reads `cannot close the last content pane`. No floating dialog.
   - Now Alt-n to split a second pane on the right → focus the second pane → close it.
   - **Expect**: closes normally (count was 2, now 1).
   - Focus the remaining pane → try to close again.
   - **Expect**: refusal again.
   - Sanity: with two right-side panes and a running command in one, close that running pane.

4. **Sidebar still refused absolutely**: focus any of the five plugin panes → press close → existing "cannot close the sidebar" refusal still works (regression check).

1. `just check` — `cargo check --workspace` plus `cargo check` on the plugin with `--target wasm32-wasip1`.
2. `just clippy` — same coverage but with `-D warnings`.

If clippy surfaces errors in code you didn't touch, call them out as pre-existing and do not auto-fix unless the user asks. Only fix warnings introduced by the current change.

Report format:
- One line per recipe: PASS or FAIL with a brief summary.
- For failures: include the first error block verbatim (file:line + message) and your read on whether it's from this session's changes or pre-existing.

Skip `just test` unless the user asks — it's slower and the user may want to gate test runs on a clean check/clippy first.

input.command:
git add crates/panopt-zellij/src/main.rs && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Refuse closing the last right-side pane (#96)

The sidebar swap-in-place model assumes a content slot exists; closing
the last visible right-side pane left no way to summon one back. The
close-focus gate now refuses absolutely when the focused tab has only
one visible terminal pane, mirroring the existing sidebar refusal.
EOF
)" && git status
input.description: Stage and commit the last-pane gate
